cover.title: your Claude seats aren't enough
cover.sub: A copilot autocompletes in the editor. That's maybe 10% of what coding agents can do. The gap to the other 90% isn't a license.

slides.title: a copilot finishes your line
slides.body: It lives in the editor, completes code, answers about the open file. Real, useful, and roughly 10% of the capability.

slides.title: an operator runs the work
slides.body: Agents that take a task to production: plan, edit across files, test, open the PR. That's the other 90%.

slides.title: the gap is operating, not licensing
slides.body: More seats don't cross it. Context, orchestration, observability, review gates, and team habits do.

slides.title: devs reach for it like autocomplete
slides.body: Trained on a copilot, teams use agents like one and get copilot results. Operating them is a different muscle.

cta.title: cross the gap on your team
cta.sub: A straight read of your agent setup in your real repo. We map what's between copilot seats and agents in production, plan yours to keep.
